commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f78e6259f470880cebcf10adf150d22a054a0caa)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an optional OpenShell plugin `env` map, passes entries to `openshell sandbox create --env`, and updates the manifest, tests, and OpenShell docs.

PR surface: Source +56, Tests +14, Docs +28. Total +98 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding from source: the PR runtime schema rejects invalid/reserved env keys, while the manifest JSON schema only constrains values to strings. The requested env passthrough itself is a feature request, not a broken existing behavior contract.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs maintainer acceptance of the new config surface plus contributor real behavior proof; automation cannot supply the contributor's OpenShell setup proof.

target.title: feat(openshell): add non-secret env config for sandbox creation
target.body: ## What Problem This Solves OpenShell sandboxes often need non-sensitive environment variables (API base URLs, model names, regions) at creation time. The OpenShell CLI supports `--env KEY=VALUE` since v0.0.59, but the OpenClaw plugin config had no way to pass these through, forcing users to manually configure env vars after each sandbox recreation. ## Why This Change Was Made Added an optional `env` map to the OpenShell plugin config. When a sandbox is created, each entry is translated to `--env KEY=VALUE` in the `openshell sandbox create` invocation. Empty by default (`{}`), so existing configs are unaffected. Env keys are validated at the schema level: - Empty keys are rejected - Keys prefixed with `OPENSHELL_` are rejected (reserved by OpenShell) - Keys must match `[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*` (valid shell identifiers) - Values are arbitrary strings ## User Impact Users can configure non-secret env vars in `openclaw.json`: ```json5 { plugins: { entries: { openshell: { enabled: true, config: { env: { MODEL_BASE_URL: "https://api.example.com/v1", MODEL_NAME: "gpt-oss-20b", }, }, }, }, }, } ``` Changing env requires sandbox recreation (openclaw sandbox recreate --all). ## Evidence ## Real behavior proof - **Source proof** — `--env` logic in `extensions/openshell/src/backend.ts`: ...Object.entries(this.params.execContext.config.env).flatMap((key, value) => [ "--env", ${key}=${value}, ]), - **Schema validation proof** — env key validation in `extensions/openshell/src/config.ts`: - [P1] Env key validation added (rejects empty keys, OPENSHELL_ prefix, invalid identifiers) - [P1] Docs updated: env changes require sandbox recreation - [P2] Schema rejects invalid keys at parse time, not at sandbox create - Files changed: 5 files, +113 lines - extensions/openshell/src/config.ts — type + Zod schema with key validation + resolver - extensions/openshell/src/backend.ts — --env args in sandbox create - extensions/openshell/openclaw.plugin.json — manifest schema + uiHints - extensions/openshell/src/config.test.ts — test coverage - docs/gateway/openshell.md — config table, example, recreate guidance - Closes: #96056 ## Merge Risk - **New config surface**: `plugins.entries.openshell.config.env` adds a new openclaw.json option. This follows the existing plugin config pattern and defaults to empty (`{}`), so existing configs are unaffected. No migration needed. - **Env key validation**: Invalid keys (empty, `OPENSHELL_` prefix, non-shell-identifier) are rejected at schema parse time, not at sandbox create. Prevents silent failures. - **Sandbox recreation required**: Env is only applied at sandbox create. Changing env without recreating has no effect. Documented in the config table and recreate guidance. - **No security regression**: Env values are passed as `--env KEY=VALUE` argv to the OpenShell CLI. No secrets should be stored here (non-secret env only, as documented). - **Backward compatible**: Config is optional. Omitted entirely → same behavior as before. No changes to existing sandbox lifecycle or channel behavior.
target.author: wm0018
